reasoning: Exchange rates over short horizons closely approximate a random walk, placing the base probability of an increase near 0.5. Over the past year, the USD/MXN rate has exhibited a strong downward trend, falling from roughly 20.45 in March 2025 to 17.22 by late February 2026. The short-term momentum from FRED over the last month also shows a negative slope (-0.009/day). This recent momentum suggests a slightly higher likelihood of a further decrease in the near term (March 8). However, analyst consensus from late 2025 projected the exchange rate to revert closer to 19 pesos per dollar by the end of 2026, meaning that at ~17.20, the peso may be perceived as overvalued and susceptible to mean reversion (an increase in the USD/MXN rate). Because of this and the general principle that uncertainty increases with the forecast horizon, the probability for the longer-term date (March 31) moves closer to 0.5.
